title: Ο περί Προστασίας Κρίσιμων Υποδομών Νόμος
description: This Law establishes the national framework for the identification, designation, and protection of critical infrastructure in the Republic of Cyprus, implementing Council Directive 2008/114/EC on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures. It defines critical infrastructure sectors, establishes procedures for operator security planning, and designates the competent national authority responsible for coordinating critical infrastructure protection activities. The Law provides the legal basis for information sharing, sectoral cooperation, and cross-border coordination with other Member States.

provisions.title: Article 1. Purpose and Scope
provisions.content: The purpose of this Law is to establish a national framework for the protection of critical infrastructure in the Republic of Cyprus, in accordance with the requirements of Council Directive 2008/114/EC on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures and the assessment of the need to improve their protection. The Law applies to infrastructure in the energy and transport sectors identified as having essential importance for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being of the population, and the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant cross-border impact on at least two Member States. The Law further applies to other sectors of critical national infrastructure as determined by the competent authority.

provisions.title: Article 2. Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 'critical infrastructure' means an asset, system or part thereof located in Member States which is essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being of the people, and the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact in a Member State as a result of the failure to maintain those functions; 'European critical infrastructure' means critical infrastructure located in Member States the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact on at least two Member States; 'critical infrastructure operator' means an owner or operator of designated critical infrastructure; 'Operator Security Plan' means the document prepared by a critical infrastructure operator identifying relevant assets and the security solutions in place or being implemented for their protection.

provisions.title: Article 3. Competent Authority
provisions.content: The Ministry of Interior is designated as the competent national authority for the implementation of this Law and for coordinating critical infrastructure protection activities across all relevant sectors. The competent authority shall maintain and regularly update the national register of designated critical infrastructure, shall coordinate the designation process in cooperation with relevant sectoral ministries and competent authorities, and shall serve as the national point of contact for critical infrastructure protection matters with the European Commission and the competent authorities of other Member States. The competent authority shall establish a national critical infrastructure protection plan setting out strategic objectives and operational frameworks for the protection of designated infrastructure.

provisions.title: Article 4. Critical Infrastructure Sectors
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law, the following sectors are identified as critical infrastructure sectors: energy, including electricity production, distribution, and supply, and oil and gas systems; transport, including road and rail networks, air transport, and maritime infrastructure; water supply and distribution; information and communication technology infrastructure; banking and financial market infrastructure; health sector, including hospitals and healthcare facilities; food production and supply chains; and government and public administration facilities. The competent authority may, in consultation with relevant sectoral bodies, designate additional sectors or sub-sectors as critical infrastructure sectors where their disruption would have a significant impact on the functioning of the state or the well-being of the population.

provisions.title: Article 5. Designation Procedure
provisions.content: The process for designating critical infrastructure shall be initiated by the competent authority in consultation with relevant sectoral ministries and regulatory authorities. Infrastructure may be proposed for designation by sectoral authorities, by operators themselves, or by the competent authority acting on its own initiative. A cross-cutting criterion based on the significance of the potential impact of disruption shall be applied in the designation process, taking into account casualties, economic effects, public confidence effects, and political effects. Designation decisions shall be communicated to operators and shall be reviewed at regular intervals, and in any case following significant changes to the relevant infrastructure or the threat environment.

provisions.title: Article 6. Operator Security Plans
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure shall prepare and maintain an Operator Security Plan identifying the critical infrastructure assets, conducting a risk assessment covering principal threats to those assets, and identifying, selecting, and prioritising counter-measures and procedures for each asset. The Operator Security Plan shall be submitted to the competent authority within a specified period following designation and shall be updated at least every two years or following significant changes to the infrastructure or the threat environment. Operators shall designate a Security Liaison Officer as the point of contact with the competent authority for security matters relating to the designated infrastructure.

provisions.title: Article 7. Security Liaison Officers
provisions.content: The Security Liaison Officer designated by each critical infrastructure operator shall serve as the primary point of contact between the operator and the competent authority for all matters relating to the protection of the designated infrastructure. The Security Liaison Officer shall have access to security-sensitive information relating to the infrastructure and shall be subject to appropriate vetting procedures as determined by the competent authority. The competent authority shall maintain a register of Security Liaison Officers and shall establish procedures for regular communication, including the convening of sectoral forums at which operators and relevant authorities may exchange information on threats and protective measures.

provisions.title: Article 8. Risk Assessment and Management
provisions.content: The competent authority shall carry out national-level risk assessments for the critical infrastructure sectors identified under this Law, integrating sector-specific assessments with cross-sectoral analysis of interdependencies and cascading effects. Operators shall conduct asset-level risk assessments as part of the Operator Security Plan preparation process and shall communicate the results to the competent authority in accordance with prescribed formats. Risk assessments shall take into account physical security threats, information security threats, and natural hazards, and shall consider both the probability of occurrence and the potential impact of identified risks. The competent authority shall issue guidance on risk assessment methodologies consistent with national and European standards.

provisions.title: Article 9. Information Sharing and Confidentiality
provisions.content: Information relating to the designation of critical infrastructure and to the security measures in place or being implemented shall be treated as sensitive and shall be subject to appropriate confidentiality protections. The competent authority shall establish procedures for the secure exchange of sensitive information between relevant national authorities and operators, and for the sharing of threat intelligence relevant to critical infrastructure protection. Information shared between the competent authority of the Republic of Cyprus and the competent authorities of other Member States relating to European critical infrastructure shall be subject to equivalent confidentiality protections, and shall not be disclosed to third parties without the consent of the originating authority.

provisions.title: Article 10. Cybersecurity of Critical Infrastructure
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure shall implement cybersecurity measures proportionate to the risk presented, in accordance with requirements established by the competent authority in coordination with the national cybersecurity authority. Cybersecurity requirements shall address network and information system security, incident detection and response, access control, and supply chain security, and shall take account of applicable Union law including Directive (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2) and implementing acts. Operators shall notify the competent authority and the national cybersecurity authority of significant cybersecurity incidents affecting designated infrastructure without undue delay, in accordance with the reporting requirements established under applicable legislation.

provisions.title: Article 11. Physical Security
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure shall implement physical security measures addressing access control to critical sites and facilities, perimeter security, detection of unauthorised access or tampering, and protective measures for critical equipment and systems. Physical security requirements shall be proportionate to the threat level and the potential impact of disruption to the designated infrastructure. The competent authority may issue sector-specific guidance on minimum physical security standards and may require operators to demonstrate compliance through audits or inspections. Operators shall maintain records of physical security incidents and shall report significant incidents to the competent authority in accordance with prescribed procedures.

provisions.title: Article 12. Business Continuity and Resilience
provisions.content: Operators of designated critical infrastructure shall develop and maintain business continuity plans addressing the measures to be taken in the event of a disruption to their critical assets, including arrangements for the continuation or rapid restoration of essential services. Business continuity plans shall be tested at regular intervals and shall be updated to reflect changes in the infrastructure, the threat environment, or the outcomes of exercises. The competent authority may coordinate sector-wide or cross-sector exercises to test collective resilience and to identify opportunities for improvement in the response to major disruptions affecting critical infrastructure.

provisions.title: Article 13. Cross-Border Coordination
provisions.content: The competent authority shall cooperate with the competent authorities of other Member States and with the European Commission in matters relating to the identification, designation, and protection of European critical infrastructure. Where infrastructure designated as critical infrastructure in the Republic of Cyprus may have a significant cross-border impact in one or more other Member States, the competent authority shall notify the relevant authorities of those Member States and shall engage in bilateral or multilateral consultation. Agreements on the modalities of cooperation and information exchange may be concluded with the competent authorities of other Member States subject to applicable legal requirements.

provisions.title: Article 14. National Contact Point
provisions.content: The competent authority shall serve as the national contact point for critical infrastructure protection matters in the Republic of Cyprus, maintaining liaison with the European Commission and with the national contact points of other Member States. The national contact point shall coordinate the provision of information requested by the European Commission under Article 12 of Directive 2008/114/EC and shall participate in the critical infrastructure warning information network (CIWIN) established by the Commission. The national contact point shall ensure that relevant information relating to European critical infrastructure threats and protective measures is disseminated to operators and relevant national authorities in a timely and secure manner.

provisions.title: Article 15. Supervisory and Enforcement Powers
provisions.content: The competent authority shall have the power to monitor compliance by operators with the obligations established under this Law, including through inspections of designated critical infrastructure, reviews of Operator Security Plans, and requests for information. Where the competent authority identifies deficiencies in an operator's security arrangements, it may issue recommendations or binding instructions requiring the operator to take corrective action within a specified timeframe. Operators failing to comply with binding instructions or obligations under this Law may be subject to administrative sanctions, which shall be proportionate, effective, and dissuasive. The competent authority may also impose interim measures where there is an immediate threat to the security of designated infrastructure.

provisions.title: Article 16. Research, Development, and Training
provisions.content: The competent authority shall promote research and development activities relevant to critical infrastructure protection and shall coordinate with relevant academic and research institutions, with industry, and with European and international bodies working on critical infrastructure protection. Training programmes for critical infrastructure protection professionals shall be developed and maintained, addressing both technical and organisational aspects of security management. The competent authority shall facilitate the participation of relevant national stakeholders in European forums and networks dedicated to the sharing of knowledge and best practices in critical infrastructure protection.

definitions.definition: An asset, system or part thereof located in Member States which is essential for the maintenance of vital societal functions, health, safety, security, economic or social well-being of the people, and the disruption or destruction of which would have a significant impact.
An owner or operator of designated critical infrastructure.
The document prepared by a critical infrastructure operator identifying relevant assets, conducting a risk assessment, and identifying counter-measures for their protection.
The individual designated by a critical infrastructure operator to serve as the point of contact with the competent authority for security matters relating to the designated infrastructure.
